wizard.title: Add a property

wizard.modeOwnedPersonalDesc: Owner-occupied, not rented out. Track purchase & mortgage.
wizard.modeRentedDesc: You're the tenant. Track your lease, rent and deposit.
wizard.changeLater: You can change this later in the property's Details tab.
wizard.name: Name
wizard.addressHint: Start typing and pick from the suggestions to set the location on the map.
wizard.addMortgageDesc: Create a linked loan record for this property
wizard.addPurchaseCostsDesc: Agent fee, purchase tax, legal — itemised one-time costs
wizard.landlordRentNote: Rent received is stored as an informational figure on the property (no income transactions are created).
wizard.tenantNote: As a tenant, there's no purchase or mortgage — we'll track your lease and rent.
wizard.createRentExpenseDesc: Adds a monthly entry to Expenses so rent shows in your budget
wizard.switchNote: You'll switch to this property after it's created.

portfolio.emptyDesc: Add your first property to get started.
portfolio.deleteConfirmDesc: This permanently removes the property and all of its records. This cannot be undone.
portfolio.rentedNote: This is a rented property — purchase & mortgage don't apply. Your lease terms are below.

search.minChars: Type at least 2 characters to search

settings.title: Settings
settings.propertyDesc: Identity, location, and physical details of this property.
settings.purchaseDesc: Purchase price, date, and acquisition costs.
settings.householdDesc: Your profile and the people who share this household.
settings.regionalDesc: Currency, time zone, and date preferences.
settings.notificationsDesc: Choose what you're reminded about and when.
settings.integrationsDesc: Connect storage, maps, and other services.
settings.appearanceDesc: Theme and language for this browser.
settings.sidebarSectionsHint: Choose which sections appear in the navigation sidebar. Hidden pages stay accessible by URL.
settings.dataDesc: Export your data, restore demo content, or delete records.
settings.mapsHint: Provider used to display and geocode addresses.
settings.mapsKeyNotice: Address search and geocoding are unavailable until a Google Maps API key is configured. Ask your administrator, or switch to OpenStreetMap.
settings.mapsKeyAdminHint: Set the following on the server to enable Google Maps:
settings.mapsKeyPlaceholder: Paste your Google Maps API key
settings.mapsKeyHowto: How to get an API key
settings.mapsKeyStep1: Sign in to the Google Cloud Console and create a project (or select an existing one).
settings.mapsKeyStep2: Under APIs & Services → Library, enable the Maps JavaScript API, Places API and Geocoding API.
settings.mapsKeyStep3: Under APIs & Services → Credentials, click Create credentials → API key.
settings.mapsKeyStep4: Recommended: restrict the key — set Application restrictions to your site's domain, and API restrictions to the three APIs above.
settings.mapsKeyStep5: Copy the key, paste it in the field above and save.

cat.channelsDesc: Connect the destinations that deliver your alerts. Choose what each one sends under Notifications.
cat.servicesDesc: Third-party apps and providers HomeVault works with.
cat.storageDesc: Where attachments are kept, and the files already uploaded.

settings.themeHint: Saved in your browser, applies immediately
settings.languageHint: Changes the app language and text direction
settings.propertyNameHint: Shown in the sidebar and dashboard
settings.addressHint: Used for the map on the dashboard
settings.appliesAll: Applies to all reminder types below
settings.remindExpensesHint: Before monthly or quarterly payments are due
settings.remindRepairsHint: When a repair has been pending too long

ch.title: Delivery channels
ch.desc: Choose which channels deliver your notifications. Connect them under the Integrations tab.
ch.inappHint: Show in the notification center (the bell)
ch.pushHint: Mobile push via the built-in service
ch.testBody: This is how a notification will look. (Preview only.)
ch.botInstructions: Open @HomeVaultBot in Telegram and send /link with this code to connect your account.
ch.emailCardDesc: Send notifications to an email address.
ch.whatsappCardDesc: Message a WhatsApp number (via the Business API).
ch.pushCardDesc: Mobile push via the built-in service.
ch.webpushUnavailable: Browser push isn't configured on the server.
ch.webpushNeedsSecure: Browser push needs a secure (HTTPS) connection and a supported browser. On a LAN/HTTP address it's unavailable; on iOS, add HomeVault to your Home Screen first.
ch.webpushNeedsKeys: Browser push needs VAPID keys on the server. Run `npx web-push generate-vapid-keys` and set VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY / VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY.

delivery.title: Notification delivery setup
delivery.desc: Configure the server-side credentials that let HomeVault actually send notifications. Values set here are stored securely in the database; environment variables, when present, take precedence and are shown as read-only.
delivery.secretSet: A value is saved. Leave blank to keep it.

email.title: Email (SMTP)
email.desc: Outgoing mail server used to send email notifications.

telegram.title: Telegram bot
telegram.desc: Bot token from @BotFather. Needed for both two-way chat and outbound alerts. Takes effect after a restart.

webpush.title: Browser push (VAPID)
webpush.desc: VAPID keypair for browser Web Push. Generate one here or paste your own.
webpush.subject: Subject (mailto: or URL)
webpush.generated: New VAPID keys generated and saved

whatsapp.title: WhatsApp (Cloud API)
whatsapp.desc: Meta WhatsApp Business Cloud API credentials.

push.title: Mobile push (Forge)
push.desc: Built-in push service credentials. Also power the AI, voice and maps features.

general.title: General
general.desc: Shared settings used to build links in emails and to register the Telegram webhook.

settings.syncEventsHint: Push HomeVault events to Google Calendar

storage.title: Where files are stored
storage.desc: Choose where HomeVault keeps the files you attach to expenses, repairs and more. Local disk needs no setup and is ideal for self-hosted and Home Assistant installs; S3-compatible storage (Cloudflare R2, Backblaze B2, AWS S3, MinIO) suits cloud setups; Google Drive uses your own account. Existing files keep working when you switch.

local.label: Local disk
local.hint: Files are stored on this server's filesystem. Use a persistent, writable path — a Docker volume, or the Home Assistant add-on's /data directory (otherwise files are lost on update).
local.fromEnv: Currently set via the STORAGE_DIR environment variable.

s3.label: S3-compatible storage
s3.hint: Works with Cloudflare R2, Backblaze B2, AWS S3 and MinIO. Paste the endpoint, bucket and access keys from your provider.
s3.fieldsRequired: Endpoint, bucket and access key ID are required
s3.secretRequired: Secret access key is required for initial setup

gdrive.hint: Configure and connect Google Drive in the File storage panel below, then make it active here.

fileStorage.title: Google Drive
fileStorage.desc: When you attach a file to an expense, repair, upgrade, loan, wishlist item or purchase cost, HomeVault uploads it to your Google Drive — never to a HomeVault server. Files live in a folder named {{folder}} and are streamed back to your browser through this app so they stay private.
fileStorage.permissionDesc: drive.file — the least-privilege scope. HomeVault can only see and manage files it created itself; it cannot read or list anything else in your Drive. Revoke any time at
fileStorage.adminOnly: Only admin users can connect or disconnect file storage. Ask the property owner.
fileStorage.notConfiguredHint: The deployment is missing the Google OAuth client ID/secret. Until they're added, file uploads will fail.
fileStorage.howToStep1: In Google Cloud Console (console.cloud.google.com), open or create a project, then enable the Google Drive API: APIs & Services → Library → search "Google Drive API" → Enable.
fileStorage.howToStep2: Set up the OAuth consent screen: APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen → External. While in Testing mode, add your Google account as a Test User.
fileStorage.howToStep3: Create a credential: APIs & Services → Credentials → Create credentials → OAuth 2.0 Client ID → Web application. Add this as an Authorized redirect URI:
fileStorage.howToStep4: Copy the Client ID and Secret, then set these env vars on the server and restart:
fileStorage.reconnectHintWithEmail: Google revoked HomeVault's access to {{email}}. Uploads are failing until you reconnect.
fileStorage.reconnectHintNoEmail: Google revoked HomeVault's access. Uploads are failing until you reconnect.
fileStorage.reconnectBannerBody: The refresh token was revoked or has expired. Click Reconnect to re-grant access.
fileStorage.disconnectConfirm: Disconnect Google Drive? Existing uploads stay reachable until the file row is deleted.
fileStorage.connectedHintWithEmail: Connected as {{email}}. New uploads go to this account's Drive.
fileStorage.connectedHintNoEmail: Connected. New uploads go to the authorised account's Drive.
fileStorage.notConnectedHint: Click Connect to sign in to Google and authorise HomeVault to manage its own folder in your Drive.

manualToken.toggle: Having trouble with the OAuth flow? Use a refresh token instead.
manualToken.label: Connect with a refresh token
manualToken.hint: Use Google's OAuth Playground to generate a refresh token and paste it here — no consent screen setup needed.
manualToken.step1: Go to developers.google.com/oauthplayground and click the ⚙ gear icon.
manualToken.step2: Check "Use your own OAuth credentials" and enter your Client ID and Secret.
manualToken.step3: In the scope box type https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.file, click "Authorize APIs" and sign in with your Google account.
manualToken.step4: Click "Exchange authorization code for tokens" and copy the refresh_token value.

creds.label: OAuth credentials
creds.setupHint: Enter your Google OAuth 2.0 client credentials to enable file uploads.
creds.fromEnvHint: Credentials are set via GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID / GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET / GOOGLE_OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI. Edit your .env file to change them.
creds.redirectUriHint: Must exactly match an Authorized redirect URI registered in Google Cloud Console (OAuth 2.0 Client ID → Web application). Google only allows localhost for local installs — not LAN IPs like 192.168.x.x.
creds.secretPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep the saved secret
creds.secretSaved: A secret is already saved. Leave blank to keep it.
creds.secretRequired: Client Secret is required for initial setup

storedFiles.summaryHintProperty: Files attached to anything in the currently-selected property.
storedFiles.summaryHintAll: All files you've uploaded, across every property + any legacy files from before per-property folders.

settings.exportFilesZipHint: Downloads every file you uploaded. Pair with the JSON export above before disconnecting Google Drive.

auth.resetLinkSent: If an account exists for {{email}}, a reset link is on its way. Check your inbox.
auth.resetMissingToken: This reset link is missing its token.
auth.passwordUpdated: Your password has been updated. You can now sign in.

admin.noAccess: You don't have access to the admin console.
admin.title: Admin console
admin.subtitle: Server-wide users, workspaces, and configuration.
admin.deploymentSaas: Cloud, multi-tenant: open registration and tenant isolation.
admin.deploymentStandalone: Single install: invite-only, preserves standalone behaviour.
admin.saasIncompatibleNoAuth: SAAS is incompatible with NO_AUTH mode.
admin.openRegistrationDesc: Allow anyone to create an account. Invited users can always join.
admin.requireEmailVerificationDesc: Block sign-in until the address is confirmed
admin.userLoginOn: On — everyone must sign in with their own email and password.
admin.userLoginOff: Off — anyone who reaches this server is automatically signed in as a single admin (admin@local) with no password.
admin.noCredentialedAdminWarning: Create a super-admin with a password first (Users → New user → grant super-admin). Otherwise enabling login would lock you out of the console.
admin.userLoginOnHint: Turn this off to restore the automatic admin login.
admin.userLoginOffHint: Turn this on once at least one super-admin has a password.
admin.createUserDesc: Provision an account directly. The user can sign in with this email and password immediately — no verification email is required.
admin.namePlaceholderDerive: Derived from the email if left blank
admin.workspacePlaceholder: A personal workspace is created if left blank
admin.deleteWorkspaceConfirm: Permanently delete "{{name}}" and ALL its data? This cannot be undone.
admin.billingProvider: Billing provider: {{provider}}. Capabilities are gated by plan in SAAS mode; everything is included in standalone.
admin.resetPasswordHint: Set a new password for this user. Their existing sessions will be signed out.
admin.confirmDeleteUser: Delete this user permanently? Their workspaces are kept; this can't be undone.
admin.name: Name
